raw_consumer_statement: On XX/XX/year>, a charge was made to XXXX XXXX on my husband 's ( an authorized user ) card. We were asleep at that time in XXXX, so we did not authorize this. When I received the alert that it was posted to my account, I called immediately. It was XX/XX/year>. The rep stated she saw a {$500.00} charge to XXXX XXXX that she had already taken care of. I said this was the {$330.00} to XXXX XXXX and was already posted. Anyway, I assumed she took care of both, but didn't. She canceled the card so I had no way to dispute it online. It wasn't until XXXX calls later, through all of XXXX, that I finally found out that it had never been done. Since then, the company checked with XXXX XXXX who said since the person making the charge was allowed onto the plane with correct ID, that the charge stands. BUT we NEVER authorized the charge. Just because the airline checked the person 's ID, doesn't mean the ticket was purchased correctly. It was purchased through fraud. This person, a XXXX XXXX, fraudulently used my husband 's card, our address, and an email address that was extremely similar to his. This is fraud, clear and simple, but the credit card company is wanting me to pay the full amount rather than the legal amount of {$50.00} allowed by law. Plus, I believe someone should attempt to stop this XXXX XXXX. This obviously was well planned. To purchase a ticket and then be on a plane XXXX hours later while the legitimate card owner is asleep was well planned. This person tried to buy XXXX tickets, but only got away, on our card at least, with XXXX of them. This XXXX XXXX needs to do a better job not allowing fraudulent charges to even happen. And I want them to remove this charge from my account or charge the {$50.00} maximum amount allowed by law because this is INDEED fraud. They have never explained to me how ( text, email, phone call ) I or my husband " authorized '' this charge from XXXX miles away while asleep.
